title: Wapol Learns About the Castle
description: On pages 1–4, wapol learns about the castle as wapol learns that the Straw Hats are heading for Drum Castle, while Dalton and the villagers face the threat of the old tyrant’s return.
scene: Wapol Learns About the Castle. Wapol learns that the Straw Hats are heading for Drum Castle, while Dalton and the villagers face the threat of the old tyrant’s return. The focus is the story beat, character decisions, and dialogue driving this part.

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: The heck are they doing now?

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: They stopped chasing after us,

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: But now they're up to something else...

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: W-Wait...

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: It can't be...

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: They couldn't possibly be trying to...!?

dialogue.speaker: Kuromarimo
dialogue.text: All those who can fight, grab your weapons and head for big horn!

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: Hurry!

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: to big horn!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Follow after Dalton!

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: Wapol has returned! Grab your arms and prepare to fight!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: It's time to protect our country!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Take arms and head for big horn!

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: Hey, VIV!!

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: You sure this is the right way to Gyasta?

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: I think so but I'm not entirely sure...

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: You're not sure? That's not gonna cut it, princess.

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: Just imagine what'll happen when the guys reach the mountaintop,

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: Only to realize the doctor's missing! Who knows what they'll do then!

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: Come on, you're a princess. Figure something out.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: What does me being a princess have to do with anything!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Why don't you take a look at this map and figure something out?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: There's nothin' but snow out here! I wouldn't be able to tell apart north from South even with that map!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Well...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: There should be a sign that points to Gyasta along our way so just keep an eye out for that.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: gyasta

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Got it!

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: GULP!

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: Deeee- Eeeeeee- Eeeel- Icious!

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: Listen well, my citizens! Everything that exists in this country is nothing but food for me!

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: Kuro- Marimo! Tell the people why I'm so great!

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: The fact that you are King proves your greatness, your majesty.

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: Ahhh... nothing like a nice roasted house cooked to well-done on a cold snowy day!

summary: This part centers on wapol learns about the castle, clarifying the immediate conflict and how it moves the chapter toward its next major turn.
